title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
summary: Nupur Sharma discusses the U-curve problem where agents mishandle middle context in large input windows. She covers iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as solutions, then introduces an 80/20 orchestration split using high-reasoning models for discovery and lighter deterministic models for validation, demonstrated in Qodo's code review system.
writeup: Nupur Sharma of Qodo argues that larger context windows do not fix the performance degradation agents suffer on long contexts, a pattern she calls the U-curve effect where information at the middle of the context gets neglected. The talk proposes iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as remedies for managing what reaches the model. It also describes an 80/20 model split: reasoning-capable models handle discovery, and lighter deterministic models handle validation.
